name: dev-rfc-review-ui
prompt: I've got this skill at skills/.experimental/dev-rfc/ that generates an HTML review page for RFC documents. The review template at assets/review_template.html renders user-submitted RFC markdown and displays it in the browser. Can you do a security analysis of this skill? I want to understand what could go wrong.
expected_output: Evidence-backed threat model with data flow traces, pattern clustering if applicable, and exploit chains.
assertions: Output has all 6 sections: Overview, Trust boundaries, Attack surfaces, Systemic findings (or omitted if <3 similar), Exploit chains (or omitted if none), Criticality calibration
Contains at least one data flow trace showing entry point → function chain → sink with validation status at each step
The XSS finding includes a specific trace: markdown content → marked.parse() → innerHTML, with line numbers or code references
Trust boundaries classify RFC markdown content as attacker-controlled input
If multiple XSS-class findings exist, they are grouped into a systemic finding with a shared root cause
Criticality calibration rates findings with context-aware severity (localhost deployment noted)
At least one attacker story references specific code behavior, not just generic vulnerability description
